story.tasks:
Task 1: Enhance prisma/seed.ts with comprehensive seed data - Subtask 1.1: Read existing seed.ts from Epic 1 - Subtask 1.2: Enhance User seed (10 users with realistic data) - Subtask 1.3: Add Role seed (3 roles: admin, manager, user) - Subtask 1.4: Add Permission seed (all core permissions from Epic 3) - Subtask 1.5: Add File metadata seed (Epic 4)
Task 2: Implement idempotency pattern - Subtask 2.1: Use upsert for all entities - Subtask 2.2: Check existing data before insert - Subtask 2.3: Safe to run multiple times without errors
Task 3: Add npm run prisma:seed script - Subtask 3.1: Add script to package.json - Subtask 3.2: Test seed command locally - Subtask 3.3: Verify script works with .env.development
Task 4: Docker Compose seed integration - Subtask 4.1: Test: docker-compose exec app npm run prisma:seed - Subtask 4.2: Optional: Add auto-seed to docker-compose.yml - Subtask 4.3: Document seed workflow in README.md

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. prisma/seed.ts enhanced with comprehensive seed data (Epic 1'den) 2. Additional seed data created: - 10 sample users (different roles) - All core permissions (Epic 3) - 3 sample roles (admin, manager, user) - Sample files metadata (Epic 4) 3. Seed idempotent (tekrar çalıştırılabilir - re-runnable without errors) 4. Seed command available: npm run prisma:seed 5. Docker Compose integration: Seed after migration - Command: docker-compose exec app npm run prisma:seed - Auto-seed option in docker-compose.yml (optional)

doc.title: Epic 10: Development Environment
doc.snippet: Story 10.4 focuses on providing seed scripts for development data. AC includes: prisma/seed.ts enhanced, 10 sample users, all core permissions, 3 roles, sample files metadata. Dependencies: Story 10.3

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Development Environment
doc.section: Data Models and Contracts → Seed Data Entities
doc.snippet: Seed Data Entities from Epic 1, 2, 3: User Entity (Epic 2) with UUID, domainID, phone, passwordHash, etc.; Role Entity (Epic 3) with name, domainID, description; Permission Entity with resource and action; File Metadata (Epic 4) with S3 key, filename, mimeType

doc.title: Story 10.3: Environment Variable Management
doc.snippet: Environment management complete with .env.example, .env.development, config validation in src/main.ts. Database configuration ready with DATABASE_URL for Docker services

doc.title: Testing Strategy
doc.snippet: Minimum 70% test coverage enforced. Coverage breakdown: Utilities 100%, Services 80%+, Controllers 70%+, Repositories 70%+. Pattern: Arrange-Act-Assert

file.reason: Existing seed script with 2 users (admin, test) and 4 USERS permissions. Uses upsert pattern for idempotency. Needs enhancement for 10 users, roles, all permissions
Prisma schema defining User, Role, Permission entities with proper relationships for seed data generation
Already has prisma:seed script: ts-node prisma/seed.ts. Docker integration available via npm scripts
All required environment variables documented including DATABASE_URL for seed connection
PostgreSQL 15, MongoDB 6 services configured with environment variable injection. Seed script will use these services

package.name: @prisma/client
package.reason: Prisma client for database access in seed script

package.name: prisma
package.reason: Prisma ORM for database schema and migrations

package.name: bcrypt
package.reason: Password hashing for seed user accounts (10 rounds)

constraints.constraint: Use .env.development for database connection (DATABASE_URL). All Epic 1-9 environment variables must be available for seed script
Seed data must use default domainID: 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000 for multi-tenancy consistency
Passwords for development only. Use bcrypt hash with 10 rounds. No secrets in committed code
All seed operations must use upsert pattern. Safe to run multiple times without errors or duplicates
Seed must work via: docker-compose exec app npm run prisma:seed. Database services must be healthy before seed

tests.standards: Jest testing framework with Arrange-Act-Assert pattern. Minimum 70% coverage required (80%+ for services, 70%+ for controllers/repositories). Unit tests use @nestjs/testing module with Test.createTestingModule(). Seed script should be tested for idempotency and data integrity. Use ts-jest for TypeScript support.
tests.locations: Test files in __tests__ directories alongside source files. E2E tests in /test directory. Mock factories available from Story 9.6. Seed data can be used in test setup.

test.description: Test that seed script runs successfully without errors
Run seed twice and verify no duplicates or errors
Verify 10 users, 3 roles, and all permissions are created
Test seed works via docker-compose exec command
Verify seed uses correct DATABASE_URL from .env.development
